summary: Iteration 15 — AI Life Advisor Chatbot end-to-end testing. Backend: 10/10 pytest cases PASS (18.87s) covering (1) /api/chat financial mode → string response + chat-* session_id; (2) GET /api/chatbot/conversations contains session with mode='financial', message_count=2, title matches first user message; (3) second message in same session_id → message_count=4; (4) legal mode response contains '⚖' or 'attorney'; (5) general mode response does NOT contain attorney disclaimer; (6) search 'finance' returns ≥1 result with 'finance' substring; (7) search 'a' (len<2) returns empty; (8) GET /api/chatbot/quick-actions returns exactly the 8 spec prompts in order; (9) DELETE /api/chatbot/conversations/{sid} → 200 ok, session no longer in list; (10) DELETE /api/chatbot/conversations → 200 with messages_deleted+conversations_deleted counts, subsequent GET returns empty list. Frontend (Playwright mobile 390x900, logged in test1@plos.app/test123 → More → module-chatbot): STEP 11 PASS — AI Life Advisor header renders w/ brain icon, 'Online · Life Advisor' status, chatbot-mode + chatbot-history buttons. STEP 12 PASS — all 8 quick-action chips (quick-0..quick-7) render; tap quick-0 produced right-aligned user bubble, AI reply with copy-{id} button. STEP 13 PASS — chatbot-mode sheet shows all 5 modes; tapping mode-legal switches header to 'Online · Legal Advisor'. STEP 14 PASS — sending a legal text message yields response containing 'attorney'/'⚖' disclaimer. STEP 15 PASS — history drawer opens with 'Conversations' title and ≥1 conv-{sid} entry. STEP 16 PASS — typing 'finance' in history-search renders search-result-0. STEP 17 PASS — history-new-chat closes drawer, resets to greeting + quick-action chips + 'Life Advisor' label. STEP 18 PASS — tapping del-{sid} (with window.confirm auto-accepted) removes the conv row. STEP 19 PASS (degenerate) — after STEP 18 the list was empty so clear-all-conversations not rendered; verified empty state. STEP 22 PASS — sent markdown probe ('**Bold text**', `- item one`, `- item two`, `print('hi')` inline code); rendered output shows '•' bullets, 'Bold text', and 'print' content (see screenshot /tmp/chatbot_final.png — bullets render as • markers, code styled inline). STEP 20 FAIL — attach-btn click is a SILENT NO-OP on web. STEP 21 PARTIAL — mic-btn click does not crash but Voice unavailable branch is also silent on web. Root cause for both: react-native-web's Alert.alert is a no-op stub (Alert.alert() {} — confirmed in node_modules/react-native-web/dist/exports/Alert/index.js). Delete + clear-all paths are unaffected because chatbot.tsx already branches to window.confirm on web (lines 307, 325), but showAttachmentNotice (line 364-365) and toggleVoice unavailable branch (line 337) use only Alert.alert. No regressions detected; AI integration latency ~6-20s per Claude turn.

ui_bugs.issue: Tapping attach-btn is a SILENT NO-OP on react-native-web because Alert.alert is a stub on web. Spec STEP 20 expects 'Attachments coming soon' message to surface. Reference: /app/frontend/app/chatbot.tsx:364-365 (showAttachmentNotice → Alert.alert). Same pattern as the delete/clear-all branches (lines 307, 325) which correctly use window.confirm on web — fix is to mirror that approach (e.g. `if (Platform.OS === 'web') window.alert('Attachments coming soon — document and photo analysis will be wired up in the next iteration.'); else Alert.alert(...)`).

When Web Speech API is unavailable (e.g. Safari, Firefox, headless contexts), the user clicks mic-btn and gets no feedback because the Alert.alert at line 337 is a no-op on react-native-web. The 'available' branch works (Chrome/Edge). Reference: /app/frontend/app/chatbot.tsx:334-339. Fix: add Platform.OS==='web' branch with window.alert, OR render an inline toast/banner.

react_native_issues.issue: react-native-web ships Alert.alert as a no-op (class Alert { static alert() {} }) — confirmed in /app/frontend/node_modules/react-native-web/dist/exports/Alert/index.js. Any call to Alert.alert from chatbot.tsx that does NOT have a Platform.OS==='web' fallback will silently do nothing on the web preview. Affects attach-btn and voice-unavailable paths. Reference: /app/frontend/app/chatbot.tsx:337, 365.
Console warning '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' persists from prior iterations — comes from a dependency not from chatbot.tsx. Cosmetic.
Console warning 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Cosmetic, third-party origin.

Animated thinking-dots indicator logs '`useNativeDriver` is not supported because the native animated module is missing. Falling back to JS-based animation.' This is expected on web (no native module) and harmless, but the warning could be silenced by setting useNativeDriver:false explicitly inside chatbot.tsx's Animated.timing calls when Platform.OS==='web'.

action_items: (MEDIUM) Fix attach-btn silent no-op on web: in /app/frontend/app/chatbot.tsx:364, add a Platform.OS==='web' branch that calls window.alert('Attachments coming soon — …'), OR introduce a custom in-app Toast component used by all stub buttons. Mirror the pattern already used at lines 307/325 (deleteConversation/clearAll).
(LOW) Fix mic-btn silent failure on browsers without webkitSpeechRecognition: in /app/frontend/app/chatbot.tsx:337, replace Alert.alert with a web-aware fallback (window.alert or inline banner).
(LOW, optional) Silence the 'useNativeDriver is not supported' warning by setting useNativeDriver:false on web for the 3-dot thinking animation (Animated.timing/Animated.loop in chatbot.tsx).
(Carry-over from iter 10–14) Split /app/backend/server.py (now 5,460+ lines) into per-module routers and extract chatbot endpoints + MODE_OVERLAYS into /app/backend/routers/chatbot.py.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:2169-2235 — /api/chat correctly upserts chat_conversations on first message of each session (insert if missing, update last_message_at+mode otherwise), persists both user and assistant messages to chat_messages with role/mode/created_at, and updates last_message_at again after the AI response. The flow is atomic-ish but not transactional — if the LLM call fails after the user message has been persisted, the conversation will exist with 1 message + no AI reply. Acceptable for a chatbot but worth noting.

server.py:2239-2271 — /api/chatbot/conversations backfill loop is O(N) on chat_messages.distinct(session_id) and does a sequential find_one for each orphan. Fine for typical user volumes (<50 sessions) but should be batched if usage scales.

server.py:2288-2300 — /api/chatbot/search uses re.escape on the query, so regex metacharacters are safely treated as literals. Returns _id-excluded docs. Sort by created_at desc, limit 50 — matches spec.
server.py:2164-2166 — _conversation_title truncates at 60 chars with ellipsis. Empty/whitespace messages fall back to 'New conversation'.

chatbot.tsx:307, 325 — Delete and clear-all flows correctly check `typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.confirm(...)` on web BEFORE falling through to Alert.alert. This is the exact pattern that should be replicated for showAttachmentNotice (line 364) and the voice-unavailable branch (line 337).

chatbot.tsx:75-99 — renderInline markdown parser handles **bold**, *italic*, `code` with a single regex pass and indexed React keys. Block-level rendering (## headings, • bullets, 1. numbered lists) is presumed handled elsewhere in chatbot.tsx — verified end-to-end via STEP 22 where the rendered AI reply showed proper • bullets and inline code styling.
chatbot.tsx:411-414 — Per-AI-message Copy button uses testID `copy-${m.id}` (verified). expo-clipboard import (line 20) provides cross-platform clipboard write.

api.ts:204-216 — aiApi correctly extends chat() with optional mode param, and exposes conversations(), deleteConversation(sid), clearAllConversations(), searchMessages(q), quickActions() — matches the backend endpoints.

success_rate.frontend: 20/22 verified — 18 fully pass (steps 11-19, 22), 2 issues (step 20 attach-btn silent no-op on web due to react-native-web Alert.alert stub; step 21 mic-btn doesn't crash but Voice unavailable branch is also silent on web for browsers without webkitSpeechRecognition)

seed_data_creation: Backend tests created 3 chat sessions (financial, legal, general) during test_01/04/05, then test_09 deleted the financial session and test_10 cleared all conversations + messages. Final DB state for test1@plos.app: 0 chat_messages, 0 chat_conversations. Frontend test then created 2-3 additional sessions during STEPS 12-22 and cleaned most via STEP 18-19; one residual conversation likely remains from STEP 22 markdown probe and can be cleared via DELETE /api/chatbot/conversations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 15 verified: chatbot backend is solid (all 10 endpoints work, mode overlays produce expected behavioral differences in Claude responses, search uses re.escape so safe with regex metacharacters, backfill creates conversation rows for orphan sessions). Frontend renders correctly with brain icon, mode-tinted header (purple for Legal, green-ish for Financial, etc.), 8 quick-action chips, copy buttons per AI message, history drawer with search/delete/clear-all, and lightweight markdown parser (bold/italic/code/bullets/numbered) all working end-to-end. KEY FINDING for future testing: react-native-web's Alert.alert is a no-op stub — any place in the codebase that calls Alert.alert without a Platform.OS==='web' fallback (or without a custom Toast) will silently do nothing on the web preview. The chatbot's delete/clear-all paths handle this correctly via window.confirm; attach-btn (line 365) and mic-btn unavailable branch (line 337) do NOT. Useful Playwright patterns reused: (1) `await page.add_init_script('window.confirm = () => true;')` before nav for auto-accept on delete/clear flows. (2) `page.get_by_text('More', exact=True).first.click(force=True)` then `await (await page.query_selector('[data-testid="module-chatbot"]')).evaluate('el => el.click()')` because More-tab overlay intercepts normal clicks. (3) For long Claude calls, poll for `[data-testid^="copy-"]` (only appears on assistant messages) with a 60s budget — much more reliable than waiting for specific text.
rca_of_issue: RCA — attach-btn silent no-op (STEP 20) and mic-btn silent unavailable feedback (STEP 21): react-native-web ships Alert as `class Alert { static alert() {} }` (verified in /app/frontend/node_modules/react-native-web/dist/exports/Alert/index.js). Any Alert.alert call from a web-rendered RN component will simply return without side effects. The chatbot.tsx file already demonstrates the correct mitigation in deleteConversation (line 305-313) and clearAll (line 323-331): branch on `typeof window !== 'undefined'` and call window.confirm instead. Reproduction: tap attach-btn on the web preview — no dialog, no DOM change, no console error. Mitigation: in showAttachmentNotice (line 364) wrap as `if (Platform.OS === 'web') { window.alert('Attachments coming soon …'); } else { Alert.alert('Attachments coming soon', '…'); }` — or introduce a centralized `notify(title, body)` helper that all five Alert.alert call-sites in this file can use. Same fix applies to the voice-unavailable branch at line 337.
